user_query:
I'm adjusting my farm plan for drier springs. Please inspect the local workspace to build a practical field task plan and update my irrigation thresholds based on the recent weather trend.

Use these inputs:
- input/weather/precip_2020_2024.csv (monthly rainfall, mm)
- input/weather/frost_dates.csv (last spring frost dates)
- input/fields.csv (fields and planned crops)
- config/crops.yaml (per-crop buffers and task durations)
- config/irrigation.yaml (current moisture trigger thresholds)

Requirements:
1) Compute rainfall trend for the growing season:
   - Use April–September months.
   - Baseline: average April–September rainfall across 2020–2022.
   - Recent: average April–September rainfall across 2023–2024.
   - Percent change = (recent − baseline) / baseline × 100.
   - Threshold adjustment rule for moisture_trigger_pct (per crop):
     • If percent change ≤ −20%: increase by +4 percentage points.
     • If −20% < percent change ≤ −10%: increase by +2.
     • If percent change ≥ +10%: decrease by −2.
     • Otherwise: no change.
   - After adjustment, cap each moisture_trigger_pct to stay within [10, 35] inclusive.

2) Compute planting timing:
   - Median last spring frost date from input/weather/frost_dates.csv.
   - For each field in input/fields.csv, look up its crop in config/crops.yaml to get:
     • planting_buffer_days_after_last_frost
     • task_durations: prep_days, planting_days, irrigation_setup_days
   - Schedule per-field tasks deterministically:
     • planting_start = median_last_frost_date + planting_buffer_days_after_last_frost
     • planting_end = planting_start + planting_days − 1
     • prep_start = planting_start − prep_days
     • prep_end = planting_start − 1
     • irrigation_setup_start = planting_end + 1
     • irrigation_setup_end = irrigation_setup_start + irrigation_setup_days − 1
   - All dates in YYYY-MM-DD.

3) Deliverables and paths:
   - Write a structured plan to output/plan.json with this top-level shape:
     {
       "source_files": [list of relative paths you read],
       "rainfall_trend": {
         "baseline_years": [2020, 2021, 2022],
         "recent_years": [2023, 2024],
         "baseline_apr_sep_avg_mm": number,
         "recent_apr_sep_avg_mm": number,
         "percent_change": number,
         "threshold_adjustment_pct_points": integer
       },
       "median_last_frost_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
       "fields": [
         {
           "field_id": string,
           "field_name": string,
           "crop": string,
           "tasks": [
             {"name": "prep", "start_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "end_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "depends_on": []},
             {"name": "plant", "start_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "end_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "depends_on": ["prep"]},
             {"name": "irrigation_setup", "start_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "end_date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "depends_on": ["plant"]}
           ]
         }, ...
       ],
       "warnings": [list any crops referenced in input/fields.csv that are missing in configs]
     }
   - Modify config/irrigation.yaml in place by applying the threshold adjustment to each crop's moisture_trigger_pct (respecting the [10, 35] cap). Preserve all other keys/structure.
   - Before modifying, create a backup at config/irrigation.yaml.bak in the same directory.

Notes:
- Do not hardcode paths; discover and read the files from the specified directories. Include every file you read under source_files in output/plan.json.
- The task should be fully reproducible from the provided inputs.
- If you encounter a crop in fields.csv that is not defined in config/crops.yaml or config/irrigation.yaml, include a warning and skip scheduling/updating for that crop. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
